extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.75) and then sharply drops to a low valley (around 0.1) within the first third of the time.  
R2: After this valley, the series rises and stays roughly in a mid-range band (about 0.35–0.55) for the remaining two-thirds of the time.  
R3: The end of the series is not higher than its early peak; the final value stays at or below about 0.6.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.9) with one or more sharp early spikes in the first 5–10 time steps.  
R2: It then drops to a low valley (around 0.1) by roughly one-third of the length, similar to Class 0.  
R3: After rising from the valley to a mid-range band (about 0.35–0.55), the series shows a pronounced local peak or “bump” in this band in the second half of the time, higher and more isolated than the surrounding mid-range values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series reaches its global minimum relatively early (around 25–40% of the total time).
R2: From this early minimum, the series shows a broad, gradual rise that peaks roughly in the middle of the time axis, then stays roughly flat or slightly decreasing.
R3: In the last quarter of the series there is no strong, sustained upward trend; end values are similar to or lower than the mid‑series peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series keeps decreasing for longer, so the global minimum occurs later (around 35–55% of the total time).
R2: From this later minimum, the series rises more steeply and reaches its main peak in the second half of the time axis.
R3: The last quarter of the series maintains values near this later peak, without returning toward the early‑series low region.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 0–10), the series reaches its minimum clearly before the midpoint of the sequence (roughly before time 40).  
R2: From that early minimum up to around time 50–60, the series shows a sustained upward trend.  
R3: In the last third of the sequence, the series stays in a medium-to-high range (about 0.4–0.6) with only small fluctuations and no strong downward drift.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series continues downward longer and reaches its global minimum close to the midpoint of the sequence (roughly around time 40).  
R2: From that minimum, the series rises to a medium level (about 0.4–0.5) but does not climb as high as the early peak.  
R3: In the last third of the sequence, the series tends to drift slightly downward or remain flat, staying in a medium range without a clear late upward trend.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 10–15), the series stays relatively low and flat for a while (a broad valley, roughly below 0.25 until about time 30–35).  
R2: From that valley, the series then rises steadily into a mid‑level plateau around 0.45–0.55 (without very sharp peaks) and remains there for a long middle segment.  
R3: The final part of the series stays at or returns to that mid‑level plateau without any pronounced late spike above roughly 0.6.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series also enters a low valley, but this valley is shorter and ends earlier (it starts rising clearly before time 30).  
R2: From that earlier valley, the series rises more steeply and overshoots into a higher mid‑to‑high region, often approaching or briefly exceeding about 0.55–0.6.  
R3: The middle/late part therefore contains a more pronounced hump or peak (higher and/or sharper than in Class 0) before settling back toward mid levels.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series reaches its minimum later in time (around indices 25–40) rather than very early.  
R2: After this minimum, the series rises to a mid‑level plateau around 0.45–0.55 that persists for a long central segment.  
R3: In the final segment, the series either stays roughly flat or rises only moderately (no strong upward trend beyond the mid‑level plateau).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series quickly reaches its minimum very early (around indices 10–20) rather than later.  
R2: From this early minimum, the series shows a strong sustained upward trend, climbing from near 0.1 to about 0.45–0.55.  
R3: After reaching this higher level, the series stays around that level or slowly declines, without a further late rise above it.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (first ~10 time steps), the series reaches its minimum later, around the middle of the sequence (roughly indices 30–40), not very early.  
R2: After this central minimum, the series exhibits a clear and sustained rise, forming a broad “U”-shape over the middle of the sequence.  
R3: In the last third of the sequence (after the rise), the values stabilize at a medium level without a pronounced continued downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (first ~10 time steps), the series quickly reaches its minimum early (roughly indices 10–20), sooner than in Class 0.  
R2: From this early minimum, the series rises steeply and then stays mostly flat at a medium level (plateau) through the middle of the sequence, without a large “U”-shaped dip centered in the series.  
R3: In the last third of the sequence, the values tend to slightly decrease or stay flat, without a marked second rise compared to the middle section.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an early sharp drop, the signal reaches its minimum relatively early (around 25–40% of the series length), then rises and stays around a mid-level plateau (roughly 0.45–0.55) through the middle of the series.  
R2: In the last quarter of the series, the signal remains roughly flat or only gently trends upward, without a pronounced single peak higher than the mid-series plateau.  
R3: Overall, the highest values of the series occur at the very beginning, and are not exceeded again later in the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an early sharp drop, the signal continues downward for longer so that the global minimum occurs later (around 35–50% of the series length), followed by a clear upward rebound.  
R2: In the middle-to-late part of the series, the signal forms one or more noticeable humps/peaks above the surrounding levels (a clear “bump” rather than a flat plateau).  
R3: Compared with Class 0, the tail segment tends to slope slightly downward or remain flat after this hump, and the strongest non-initial peak typically appears in the second half of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series reaches its minimum later in time (around the middle of the sequence rather than very early).  
R2: After this minimum, the series climbs and stays mostly in a mid-range band (roughly 0.4–0.55) for a long segment.  
R3: The very end of the sequence is flat-to-slightly-rising, without a pronounced downward trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series quickly reaches its global minimum very early in the sequence.  
R2: From that early minimum, the series rises steeply and then stabilizes in a mid-range band (roughly 0.4–0.55).  
R3: The very end of the sequence is flat-to-slightly-falling, without a pronounced upward rebound.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial sharp drop, the series reaches its minimum around the first third of the time and then clearly rises.  
R2: In the middle third of the time, the series has a broad “valley” followed by a gradual increase, without a tall, narrow peak.  
R3: In the last third of the time, values stay roughly between 0.35 and 0.55 with only gentle wiggles (no spike clearly above ~0.6).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial sharp drop, the series rises and forms a noticeable peak in the middle or last third of the time, reaching at least about 0.55–0.6.  
R2: Compared with Class 0, the lowest point in the first third is not followed by a long flat valley; instead, the rise to the middle peak is steeper and more pronounced.  
R3: In the last third of the time, values oscillate around 0.4–0.5 but typically descend slightly or stay flat after the main peak, without a sustained late upward trend.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply to a low valley near 0.1–0.15 within the first third of the time.  
R2: After this early valley, the series rises and stays mostly in a mid range (roughly 0.35–0.55) for the middle part of the time.  
R3: In the last quarter of the time, the series shows a clear upward drift or bump, ending higher than the mid-range plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (around 0.7–0.9) and drops sharply, but the low valley is shallower or shorter than in Class 0 (it does not stay near 0.1 for long).  
R2: After this drop, the series rises to a mid level (around 0.35–0.55) and then gradually decreases or stays flat, without a strong upward bump near the end.  
R3: The final values end at or below the mid-level plateau, typically not forming a late increasing trend as in Class 0.
